387.reason: [This code assumes that every '&' is followed by another '&', but if a single '&' appears (which is a lexical error), it will incorrectly consume the next character and output "&&" as token AND, instead of reporting error type 'a'. This contradicts the requirement that standalone '&' should be flagged as an error.]

392.reason: [Similarly, this code assumes every '|' is followed by another '|'. If a single '|' appears, it will incorrectly consume the next character and output "||" as token OR, instead of reporting error type 'a'.]
